description:
RFC 7523, Section 2.1, Using JWTs as a means for requesting an OAuth 2.0 access token.
The following example demonstrates an access token request with a JWT as an authorization grant :

POST /token HTTP/1.1
grant_type=urn:ietf:params:oauth:grant-type:jwt-bearer&assertion=|signed-JWT-token|

grantType.title: Grant Type
grantType.description: OAuth 2.0 grant type. The client uses an extension grant type by specifying the grant type value of the "grant_type" parameter of the token endpoint.

publicKeyResolver.title: Public Key resolver
publicKeyResolver.description: Define how token (JWT) will be verify

titleMap.GIVEN_KEY: GIVEN_KEY: You must provide a signature key (PEM format or HMAC secret key)
titleMap.JWKS_URL: JWKS_URL: Retrieve JWKS from URL (Basically, URL ending with '/.well-known/jwks.json')

signature.title: Signature
signature.description: Define how token has been signed.

titleMap.HS256: HS256 - HMAC with SHA-256, requires 256+ bit secret
titleMap.HS384: HS384 - HMAC with SHA-384, requires 384+ bit secret
titleMap.HS512: HS512 - HMAC with SHA-512, requires 512+ bit secret
titleMap.ES256: Elliptic Curves - ECDSA using P-256 and SHA-256
titleMap.ES384: Elliptic Curves - ECDSA using P-384 and SHA-384
titleMap.ES512: Elliptic Curves - ECDSA using P-512 and SHA-512

publicKey.title: Resolver parameter
publicKey.description: Set the signature key (GIVEN_KEY) or a JWKS URL (JWKS_URL) following selected resolver. If selected (GIVEN_KEY), must start with key type (e.g. ssh-rsa).

token_claim.title: Token claim
token_claim.description: Claim that will be added to End-User claims
